generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance PermanentMagnetSynchronousMotor : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Permanent magnet synchronous motor driving the first axis of MABI MAX 100 robot, converting electrical energy to mechanical torque (τ_M) and angular velocity (ω_M) for motion control."
]

instance GearWheel : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Mechanical gear component in the transmission system of the first axis, transmitting torque (τ_G) and angular velocity (ω_G) between motor and cycloidal gearbox."
]

instance CycloidalGearbox : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "High-precision cycloidal gearbox reducing speed and increasing torque output (τ_out) for the first axis of MABI MAX 100 robot, operating within [-90°, 90°] range."
]

instance Sinumerik840DslController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl CNC controller serving as the control interface for the first axis, enabling separate axis control with position/speed/acceleration inputs."
]

// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance MotorPositionEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Primary position encoder mounted directly on the permanent magnet synchronous motor, providing real-time angular position measurements for motor shaft."
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorPositionData
]

instance SecondaryEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Secondary position encoder located behind the gearbox, providing real-time angular position measurements for output shaft to reduce backlash and positional uncertainty."
    DTDFVocab:producedData OutputPositionData
]

instance CurrentSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Current sensor measuring input current (I_M) to the permanent magnet synchronous motor for torque calculation and dynamics analysis."
    DTDFVocab:producedData InputCurrentData
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance MotorPositionData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorPositionEncoder
]

instance OutputPositionData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SecondaryEncoder
]

instance InputCurrentData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom CurrentSensor
]

instance AxisControlData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom Sinumerik840DslController
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance SysMLBlockDefinitionDiagram : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML Block Definition Diagram representing the structural composition of the first axis (motor, gearwheel, cycloidal gearbox) and their interconnections."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLModelingTool
]

instance SysMLInternalBlockDiagram : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML Internal Block Diagram illustrating behavioral relationships between components (rotational and current flows) in the first axis."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLModelingTool
]

instance FrictionlessBaseModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MATLAB Simulink model simulating frictionless behavior of the first axis as a baseline for friction analysis."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkSimulationTool
]

instance CoulombViscousFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MATLAB Simulink implementation of Coulomb-Viscous friction model subtracted from base model to estimate friction losses in the gearbox."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkSimulationTool
]

instance StribeckFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MATLAB Simulink implementation of Stribeck friction model accounting for boundary lubrication effects in the cycloidal gearbox."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkSimulationTool
]

instance TorqueDerivationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Data calculation model deriving motor torque (τ_M = k_t × I_M) from input current (I_M) using manufacturer-provided torque constant."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataProcessingFramework
]

instance DynamicsReferenceModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Dynamics model computing reference outputs (r_M, r_G, r_out) using manufacturer-provided parameters for the first axis."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataProcessingFramework
]

instance MeasuredMotorTorqueData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Derived motor torque data (τ_M) computed from input current (I_M) and encoder measurements for real-time friction analysis."
    DTDFVocab:fromData InputCurrentData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataProcessingFramework
]

instance MeasuredOutputTorqueData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Measured output torque data (τ_out) derived from secondary encoder readings and gearbox transmission ratios."
    DTDFVocab:fromData OutputPositionData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataProcessingFramework
]

instance SimulatedFrictionlessData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Simulated reference data (r_M, r_G, r_out) representing frictionless behavior of the first axis for comparison with measured data."
    DTDFVocab:fromData FrictionlessBaseModel
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataProcessingFramework
]

instance SimulatedFrictionData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Simulated friction data (friction losses) computed by subtracting friction models from the frictionless base model."
    DTDFVocab:fromData CoulombViscousFrictionModel, StribeckFrictionModel
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataProcessingFramework
]

// C11: Enablers
instance SysMLModelingTool : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "SysML toolset used for creating structural (Block Definition Diagrams) and behavioral (Internal Block Diagrams) models of the first axis components."
    DTDFVocab:enables FrictionIdentificationService, DataAggregationService
]

instance SimulinkSimulationTool : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MATLAB Simulink environment for executing friction and dynamics simulation models (frictionless base model, Coulomb-Viscous, Stribeck)."
    DTDFVocab:enables FrictionIdentificationService, ModelDrivenInsightsService
]

instance MQTTProtocol : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MQTT-based protocol for real-time streaming of sensor data (input current, encoder readings) from physical system to Digital Shadow."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataAggregationService, VisualizationService
]

instance AssetAdministrationShell : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Asset Administration Shell providing semantic linking of data traces to models for contextual metadata management."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataAggregationService, ModelDrivenInsightsService
]

instance DataProcessingFramework : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Processing component consuming input data traces, applying simulation/data calculation models, and producing output traces with traceable computations."
    DTDFVocab:enables FrictionIdentificationService, VisualizationService
]

// C6: Services
instance FrictionIdentificationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Continuous, real-time identification of joint friction (static, kinetic, viscous) using white-box (analytical) and black-box (data-driven) models to improve motion accuracy."
    DTDFVocab:provides FrictionCharacterizationInsight, ControlAlgorithmImprovementAction
]

instance DataAggregationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Computation of higher-level abstractions (torque, angular velocity) from raw sensor data via simulation and data calculation models (e.g., MATLAB Simulink)."
    DTDFVocab:provides OperationalDiagnosticsInsight, ResearchInsightsAction
]

instance VisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Integration of processed data into a unified dashboard for real-time monitoring of physical relationships (e.g., deviations between simulated and measured data)."
    DTDFVocab:provides VisualFrictionAnalysisInsight
]

instance ModelDrivenInsightsService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Enables understanding of friction loss by comparing simulated (frictionless) and measured data, facilitating control algorithm improvements."
    DTDFVocab:provides FrictionLossQuantificationInsight, AdaptiveFrictionCompensationAction
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance FrictionCharacterizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Identification and quantification of static, kinetic, and viscous friction components in the gearbox, accounting for up to 30% of motion errors."
]

instance ControlAlgorithmImprovementAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Model-based control adjustments incorporating friction models into the robot's dynamics model for improved motion accuracy."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance OperationalDiagnosticsInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Detection of wear patterns or tool-induced changes affecting friction, enabling predictive maintenance insights."
]

instance ResearchInsightsAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Validation of white-box vs. black-box friction models for continuous identification and multi-physics analysis of joint behavior."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance VisualFrictionAnalysisInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time visualization of friction-induced deviations between simulated and measured outputs in the unified dashboard."
]

instance FrictionLossQuantificationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Quantification of friction-induced inaccuracies (up to 30% of motion errors) through comparison of simulated and measured data."
]

instance AdaptiveFrictionCompensationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Real-time adaptive compensation of friction effects in the control system based on continuously updated Digital Shadow data."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

// C12: Environment
instance FirstAxisEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Environment containing the first vertical axis components of MABI MAX 100 robot, operating within [-90°, 90°] range with max angular velocity of 75°/s."
    base:contains PermanentMagnetSynchronousMotor, GearWheel, CycloidalGearbox, MotorPositionEncoder, SecondaryEncoder, CurrentSensor, Sinumerik840DslController
]

instance DigitalShadowProcessingEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Processing environment for the Digital Shadow, integrating simulation models, data processing frameworks, and visualization tools."
    base:contains SysMLModelingTool, SimulinkSimulationTool, DataProcessingFramework, MQTTProtocol, AssetAdministrationShell
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Shadow operates on a **real-time, continuous time scale**, synchronizing with the physical system’s operational dynamics. Data is processed at **high-frequency intervals** (not explicitly quantified in the document) to enable **dynamic friction model updates**. The DS supports **sub-second latency** for data transmission (via MQTT) and processing, ensuring alignment with the robot’s motion control requirements (e.g., maximum angular velocity of 75°/s). The twinning process is **event-driven**, triggered by operational changes (e.g., wear, tool changes) or periodic recalibration of friction models."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin constellation is structured as a **purpose-driven Digital Shadow** with the following orchestration:
  1. **Data Sources**: Physical sensors (encoders, current sensors) and the Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl controller provide raw data.
  2. **Modeling Layer**: SysML models describe the **structural asset** (motor, gearboxes), while Simulink models simulate **behavioral dynamics** (friction, torque/velocity).
  3. **Processing Layer**: A **centralized Processing component** (incarnated from the Digital Shadow Reference Model) aggregates data traces, applies simulation/data calculation models, and generates higher-level abstractions (e.g., friction estimates).
  4. **Output Layer**: Processed data is visualized in a **dashboard** for friction analysis, enabling insights into **physical relationships** (e.g., backlash, elasticity) and **control algorithm improvements**.
  The constellation follows a **one-way data flow** (physical → virtual) with **no feedback to the physical system**, adhering to the Digital Shadow definition. The DS is **purpose-specific** for friction identification and does not encompass full Digital Twin capabilities (e.g., closed-loop control)."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The Digital Shadow operates primarily during the **operational phase** of the robot’s life cycle, focusing on **as-operated** representations of the first axis. It does not cover **as-designed (ideal)** or **as-manufactured** stages but instead **continuously updates** the DS during runtime to reflect **real-time friction behavior**. The DS is designed to **improve motion accuracy** by analyzing deviations between simulated (frictionless) and measured data, enabling **adaptive control adjustments** without altering the physical system’s design or manufacturing state."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The fidelity and validity of the Digital Shadow are ensured through:
  1. **Model Verification**:
    - **SysML models** are validated against the **physical structure** of the first axis (motor, gearboxes).
    - **Simulink friction models** (Coulomb-Viscous, Stribeck) are benchmarked against **manufacturer-provided dynamics parameters**.
  2. **Data Validation**:
    - **Sensor data** (encoders, current sensors) is cross-validated for consistency (e.g., encoder discrepancies indicate backlash).
    - **Reference outputs** (*r_M*, *r_G*, *r_out*) are derived using **known motor constants** and **manufacturer specifications**.
  3. **Uncertainty Handling**:
    - **Deviations between simulated (frictionless) and measured data** are analyzed to quantify friction losses.
    - **Parameter sensitivity analysis** is implied for friction models (e.g., load, temperature effects).
  4. **Traceability**:
    - **Data provenance** is maintained via **metadata** (source identification, timestamps) and **Processing component** traceability (input/output data traces and models).
    - **Uncertainty sources** (e.g., encoder noise, model approximations) are not explicitly quantified but are inferred from **data deviations**."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The technical connection between the Physical Twin (PT) and Digital Shadow (DS) involves:
  - **Data Transmission Protocol**: **MQTT** for real-time streaming of sensor data (*I_M*, encoder readings) from the Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl controller to the DS.
  - **Network Architecture**: **Cyber-Physical System (CPS) integration** where the DS acts as a **passive observer** (no feedback loop to the PT).
  - **Data Formats**:
    - **Time-series data** (e.g., *I_M*, *ω*) stored as **floating-point arrays** with associated metadata (source, timestamps).
    - **Structured models** (SysML, Simulink) exchanged via **model repositories** (e.g., MATLAB Simulink libraries).
  - **Latency**: **Low-latency** data transmission to support real-time friction analysis (exact latency not specified but implied to be sub-second)."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The Digital Shadow is **hosted in a centralized computational environment** (not explicitly specified) that integrates:
  - **Simulation Tools**: MATLAB Simulink for executing friction and dynamics models.
  - **Data Processing**: A **Processing component** (as per the Digital Shadow Reference Model) to aggregate and compute data traces.
  - **Visualization Platform**: A **unified dashboard** for real-time monitoring of friction-related metrics (e.g., torque deviations).
  Deployment involves:
  - **Hardware**: Not explicitly detailed, but assumed to include a **server or edge computing node** for real-time processing.
  - **Software Stack**:
    - **MQTT broker** for data streaming.
    - **SysML/Simulink tools** for model execution.
    - **Dashboard software** (e.g., custom-built or third-party tools like LabVIEW or Python-based dashboards)."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The Digital Shadow does not explicitly describe **horizontal integration** with external information systems or other Digital Twins. However, the **Asset Administration Shell (AAS)** framework suggests potential for integration with:
  - **Manufacturing Execution Systems (MES)** for broader process monitoring.
  - **Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP)** systems for asset lifecycle management.
  The DS is currently **purpose-isolated** for friction analysis but could theoretically extend to **cross-system data sharing** via standardized AAS interfaces."
]

instance data_ownership_and_privacy : DTDFVocab:DataOwnershipPrivacy [
    base:desc "The document does not explicitly address **data ownership** or **privacy considerations**. However, the following can be inferred:
  - **Data ownership** is likely held by the **robot operator** (e.g., MABI Robotic AG or the manufacturing facility) given the context of industrial automation.
  - **Privacy implications** are minimal for this use case, as the DS focuses on **machine-level data** (sensor readings, friction models) rather than personal or sensitive information. No mention of **data encryption**, **access controls**, or **GDPR compliance** is provided."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The Digital Shadow adheres to the following standards and frameworks:
  1. **Digital Shadow Reference Model**: Based on the **UML class diagram** (Fig. 1) and **conceptual refinements** from [15, 38, 42].
  2. **Modeling Standards**:
     - **SysML** (OMG standard) for structural and behavioral modeling.
     - **Asset Administration Shell (AAS)** (Industry 4.0 standard) for semantic asset description and data traceability.
  3. **Simulation Standards**:
     - **MATLAB Simulink** (industry-standard for dynamic systems modeling).
  4. **Data Exchange**:
     - **MQTT protocol** (IETF standard) for real-time data streaming.
  The DS does not reference **ISO/IEC 42010** (Digital Twin standards) or **IEC 62264** (enterprise control system integration) explicitly but aligns with **Industry 4.0 principles** for cyber-physical system modeling."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The document does not explicitly address **cybersecurity** or **safety considerations**. However, the following can be inferred:
  - **Security**:
    - No mention of **authentication**, **encryption**, or **firewall protections** for MQTT data transmission.
    - **No remote operation** of the physical system by the DS (consistent with Digital Shadow’s passive role).
  - **Safety**:
    - The DS is designed for **non-critical analysis** (friction modeling) and does not interact with **safety-critical control loops**.
    - **No human-machine interaction risks** are discussed, as the DS remains a **read-only** representation.
  The system assumes a **trusted operational environment** with no explicit safeguards against cyber threats or physical hazards."
]
